steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the scale shortcut: similar triangles give equal ratios
steps.content:
Since $LM \parallel PQ$, triangles $LRM$ and $QRP$ are similar (vertical angle at $R$ + alternate interior angles from parallel lines). Corresponding sides are proportional:
$$\frac{LR}{RQ} = \frac{MR}{RP} \implies \frac{7}{RQ} = \frac{6}{11}$$

steps.title: Solve for RQ, then find LQ
steps.content:
Cross-multiply: $RQ = \frac{7 \times 11}{6} = \frac{77}{6}$.
$$LQ = LR + RQ = 7 + \frac{77}{6} = \frac{42}{6} + \frac{77}{6} = \frac{119}{6}$$
That is the correct answer.
